findings.evidence: PR line 48 defines `driftForm` as `ContinuousLinearMap.smulRightL ... (innerSL ℝ b)`; Mathlib grep hits: `innerSL` at `../mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/LinearMap.lean:164`, `innerSL_apply_apply` at line 172, `smulRightL` at `../mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/Bilinear.lean:418`, and `norm_smulRightL` at `../mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/NormedSpace.lean:214`.
findings.fix: Remove `driftForm` and state later PDE lemmas directly using `ContinuousLinearMap.smulRightL ... (innerSL ℝ b)`, with local simp lemmas only if a later proof genuinely needs them.
findings.issue: `driftForm` is not new API: it is exactly Mathlib's rank-one continuous map `ContinuousLinearMap.smulRightL ℝ (EuclideanSpace ℝ n) ℝ (innerSL ℝ b)`.

findings.evidence: PR line 95 defines `massForm c` as `c • ContinuousLinearMap.mul ℝ ℝ`; Mathlib grep hits: `ContinuousLinearMap.mul` at `../mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/Mul.lean:41`, `mul_apply'` at line 45, and `opNorm_mul_le` at line 52.
findings.fix: Use `c • ContinuousLinearMap.mul ℝ ℝ` directly, relying on `ContinuousLinearMap.mul_apply'` and `opNorm_mul_le` for the pointwise and operator-norm bounds.
findings.issue: `massForm c` duplicates scalar multiplication of Mathlib's continuous multiplication map, so the definition and basic apply/add/smul/norm lemmas are parallel API under PDE names.

summary: The new pointwise form declarations are thin duplicate wrappers around existing Mathlib bundled bilinear maps, and their local apply/norm APIs recreate facts available from those constructors.
